observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to present in the SCBAA the continuing appropriations and actual amounts charged thereto;

Direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, to: 

submit an explanation or reconciliation schedule showing the differences noted between the reported actual amounts per SCBAA and SAAOB, and submit a corrected SCBAA and SAAOB, as applicable; and

reconcile the actual amounts of PS and MOOE in the SCBAA with the amounts reported in the Statement of Financial Performance, and disclose the details of reconciling items in the NTFS to provide reliable information to the users of the financial statements.

We recommended that Management instruct the:

Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to require the concerned contractors to refund the amount of ₱42,122.85; 

Municipal Engineer to strictly adhere to the provisions and guidelines in the preparation of ABC to ensure that the basis and ceiling of bid prices are acceptable/reasonable and advantageous to the government; and

BAC and TWG to ensure the correct computation of detailed estimates of the contractors during the detailed bid evaluation by taking note of the items that are not subject to OCM and Profit mark-ups.
observations.managementAction: The amounts were recovered through deductions from the claims of the concerned contractors.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to refund the fines and penalties totaling ₱70,214.57 caused by late filing and remittance of DST; and

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure regular monitoring of the deadline of filing and remittance of DST and other withholding taxes to avoid incurrence of unnecessary penalty.

We recommended that Management through its SB expedite processing of the review and approval of the revised revenue code in order to increase tax revenues and receipts that are needed to augment the budget provision for a better delivery of government services.
observations.managementAction: Management had already submitted the proposed revenue code to the SB for review and approval.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a JEV to restore the PPE accounts, particularly properties acquired in CY 2015 and prior years which were previously derecognized from the books of accounts;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to identify the individual cost of properties acquired in CY 2015 and prior years and subsequently effect the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the accurate recording of semi-expendable properties and derecognition of PPE; and
Direct the Municipal GSO and Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the required forms, registries and reports for semi-expendable properties to enhance transparency and accountability in property management.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the recorded Office Equipment amounting to ₱1,988,419.89 to their appropriate PPE account by drawing a JEV following the suggested pro-forma adjusting journal entry for proper presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

Military, Police and Security Equipment						xx
Information and Communication Technology Equipment		xx
Furniture and Fixtures						xx
Office Equipment					xx

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the DVs along with their complete supporting documents, as well as the financial transaction documents for December 2024; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that submission of monthly accounts/transactions are complete and within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Human Resource Management  Officer (HRMO) to submit the aforementioned lacking documents to support the payment of terminal leave claims; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of the required documents when processing and granting applications for terminal leave benefits to officials and employees.

We recommended that Management instruct the HRMO and Municipal Accountant to:
Allow only 30 days as the maximum number of days to be monetized pursuant to Section 22 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave; and
Require the concerned officials and employees to submit the additional documentary requirements in case of monetization of more than 50 percent of their accumulated leave credits.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC Chairman together with the Head of BAC Secretariat to:
Submit copies of the perfected contracts listed in the foregoing table with their complete supporting documents;
Strictly comply with the submission of copies of perfected contracts, approved Purchase Orders (POs), and supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period of five working days from the execution thereof for a systematic and effective review process to come up with timely, relevant audit results; and
Coordinate with the HRMO to hire/assign additional staff for the Office of the BAC Secretariat to address the lack of personnel to be able to comply with the submission of contract documents.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal GSO to:
Initiate the immediate disposal of the aforementioned unserviceable properties in accordance with established guidelines; and
Strictly observe the procedures and requirements set forth in the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties when determining the appraised values of PPE for disposal, as well as in selecting the appropriate mode of disposal to ensure transparency, efficiency, and proper asset management.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal GSO to facilitate the provision of insurance for the aforementioned motor vehicles of the Municipality with the GSIS so that a compensation may be recovered in case of loss or damage due to fortuitous events.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Submit all cancelled checks including those prior to CY 2024; and
Ensure that all copies of cancelled checks are accounted for and submitted to the Audit Team in the month they were cancelled.

We recommended that Management instruct the HRMO to seek competent and qualified personnel for the position of Zoning Officer of the Municipality pursuant to the requirements set forth in RA No. 10587 and for future similar case, carefully review and evaluate the qualifications of the personnel to be designated/appointed in any government positions.

We recommended that Management instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer being the Petty Cash Custodian to:

Ensure that payment of petty expenses is within the limit or threshold allowed not exceeding ₱15,000.00 per transaction;

Strictly utilize PCF for payment of authorized petty or miscellaneous expenses only to avoid possible misuse of government funds; and

Fully liquidate the PCF at the end of the year and a new cash advance for the said fund be set up in the ensuing year. 

We also recommended that Management issue an office order designating the Petty Cash Custodian and furnish the Audit Team a copy of the said order.

We recommended that Management:

Instruct the BAC Chairman to ensure that the winning bidder submit the DOLE approved CSHP before the approval of the contract; and

Encourage contractors to prepare and submit their CSHP with the DOLE immediately upon receipt of the NOA to ensure early release or approval prior to the start of their construction activities.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to:

Stop from recommending the award of contract in favor of an ineligible supplier and those who had a different line/ nature of business from the goods being procured; and

Validate eligibility documents submitted by the suppliers and ascertain their responsiveness by conducting site inspection of the supplier’s place of business to confirm the availability of the goods procured.  The absence of place of business operations of a prospective bidder for procurement casts doubt on the capability of the supplier to render the needed services.

We recommended that Management require the BAC to:
Submit copies of perfected contracts listed in the foregoing table with its complete supporting documents; and

Strictly comply in the submission of copies of perfected contracts, approved POs, and supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period of five working days from the execution thereof for a systematic and effective review process to come up with timely, relevant audit results.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the BAC Chairman to secure the supplier’s Permit to Sell and the LGU’s Permit to Purchase and Permit to Possess/Operate and submit the same for evaluation of the Audit Team; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that necessary documents are complete and procedures are followed first before processing the transaction for payment, for succeeding procurement of communication equipment.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Engineer to conduct monitoring of the constructed project to avoid further damage thereof and make necessary repairs on the damages/defects noted in the GSO building and warehouse; 

Require the Municipal Engineer to prepare design plans and POW to complete the unfinished infrastructure project; and

Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate a budget for the completion of the aforementioned unfinished project based on the POW to be prepared by the MEO.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, to revert immediately the unexpended balances of MOOE included in the continuing appropriations and current year appropriations for CY 2023 to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund which shall be made available for appropriation for new expenditure items in the 20 percent DF through subsequent enactment; and

Instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to stop allocating funds for PPAs classified as MOOE in the 20 percent DF.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to take up the transfer and receipt of the unexpended balance of the QRF and Mitigation Fund – MOOE of the LDRRMF in the General Fund and Trust Fund books at the end of the year when actual transfer of funds has been made for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

Likewise, we recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to immediately transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balances of 2022 DRRMF in the total amount of ₱10,325,525.76 to the Trust Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, were instructed to properly transfer the unexpended balance of the QRF and Mitigation Fund-MOOE of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to Trust Fund at the end of the year.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from obligating and charging prior year’s expenditures to current year’s appropriation; 

Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to set a deadline before the year ends for the submission of all claims of officials and employees of the LGU and other concerned payees; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record all valid claims not paid during the year as Accounts Payable subject to availability of appropriations, for fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Concerned offices were directed to coordinate for the implementation of the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management submit the locally enacted SB Resolution pertaining to the grant of communication allowance for further evaluation of the Audit Team.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Local Sanggunian revisit the locally-enacted resolution if the same is still reasonable, considering the mobile data package currently offered by the mobile networks.  Otherwise, the enjoined individuals should reduce the communication allowance to a more conscionable rate.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the grant of communication allowance be supported with necessary documents such as monthly bill of statements and certification or proof that communication allowances were used for official business only. 

Lastly, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to discontinue the deduction of loan payments against the communication allowance considering that the purpose of such allowance is to defray communication expenses.

We recommended that Management:

Require the GSO to ensure that all of its insurable properties are adequately covered by the GIF of the GSIS to avoid the risk of non-indemnification in case of damage or loss due to fortuitous events;

Instruct the GSO to submit a complete inventory of insurable properties to the GSIS for appropriate evaluation and assessment for insurance coverage, and furnish a copy of the inventory report to the Audit Team; and

Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide adequate funds for payment of the required insurance premiums to the GSIS.

We recommended that Management direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:

Collect business taxes from outside contractors who engage in projects within the municipality applying the situs of taxation.  In this regard, issue demand letters from the concerned contractors or require them to pay the accrued business taxes with the municipal government in the amount of ₱219,362.65 before being allowed to participate in the bidding process; and

Strictly observe the Municipal Ordinance enacted by the SB, particularly the collection of business taxes on business within the territorial jurisdiction to avoid non-collection of income that should accrue to the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management exerted effort in collecting all deficient business taxes from concerned contractors.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Business Permit and Licensing Officer, to:

Cause the collection of the deficient business taxes totaling ₱233,825.28 and the corresponding penalties and interests, from the suppliers concerned; and

Examine the accuracy of the declared gross receipts/sales of taxpayers by considering all available evidence upon which the tax may be based to come up with the correct assessment.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were already issued to the concerned suppliers while some had already been collected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that electronic system in monitoring and management of RPT be considered and adopted by the LGU which is a time and cost-efficient scheme in processing transactions.  It will also facilitate the preparation of reliable report on delinquent accounts of taxpayers which could be generated anytime.

We also reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to exert concerted efforts to collect the delinquent realty taxes and make necessary information drive campaign on the awareness of advance payment of real property taxes to encourage the taxpayers of prompt payment; otherwise, apply the remedies provided under the law to generate more revenue that may be appropriated for the implementation of additional programs and projects for the benefit of its constituents.

We further recommended that Management instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to advise the Punong Barangay of Barangay Buaya and its Barangay Council to inform all property owners in their barangay to coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Assessor for the change of location of real properties in their respective Tax Declarations for proper assessment of real property tax.
observations.managementAction:
Updating of RPTAR of 82 barangays is still on-going as a requirement to initiate the use of electronic system for RPT management and monitoring.

The Acting Municipal Treasurer was instructed to continue sending notices of delinquent taxpayers.

The Municipal Assessor has already informed the Punong Barangay of Buaya and its council regarding the RPT collection and the need to locate the property owners.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:

Enforce the termination of the lease contract of delinquent stall holders for failure to pay the rental fee for three consecutive months as stipulated in the Contract of Lease; 

Enforce the collection of delinquent accounts totaling ₱1,407,900.00; and

Regularly monitor and update the records on payments of the stallholders and strictly impose applicable surcharges as embodied in the Contract of Lease, and if possible, issue Statements of Account at least a month before the rent becomes due, to ensure prompt payment of rentals.
observations.managementAction: Management had started to terminate the lease contract of delinquent stall holders.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:

Update the records of the stall holders and renew all expired Contracts of Lease with stallholders/lessees who have not violated the terms and conditions of their previous contracts to enforce its incorporeal rights over the lease and impose compliance by the lessees with their obligations;

Prepare and execute the Contract of Lease for the 139 stallholders; and

Monitor all stallholders to have valid and updated contracts and implement the provisions incorporated in the Contract of Lease to protect the interest of the LGU.

We recommended that Management advise the SB to revisit/review the existing Local Revenue Code of the LGU and require the concerned department heads to prepare proposed adjustments on tax rates as prescribed/fixed under the Code and submit to the SB for approval to fully tap the scope of the taxing power of the LGU to increase tax revenues and receipts through updating of the rates in the existing tax code which will augment the budget provision for a better delivery of government services.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had conducted workshop and committee hearing during the year with the concerned offices relative to the proposed adjustments on tax rates.

accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Road Asset
Management System.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the mandatory
designation and bonding requirement of all accountable officers handling the
collection of local taxes and licenses of the Municipality pursuant to the provisions
of PD No. 1445, Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 and Republic Act No. 7160 in order
to safeguard government funds and to protect the interest of the government.

Treasurer to immediately stop the practice of allowing permanent employees from
performing collection functions unless they are duly designated as revenue collectors
and are bonded in accordance with existing regulations.

Municipal Accountant to release the RPT shares of the Barangays within five days
after the end of each quarter and shall not be subject to any lien or holdback for
whatever purpose pursuant to Section 286 (a) of RA No. 7160 in order for the
Barangays to utilize the funds for the benefit of their constituents and to avoid any
sanction in the future.

We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal
Accountant certified list of taxpayer and the amount collectible which
shall serve as basis in establishing Real Property Tax and Special
Education Tax Receivables at the beginning of the year and to recognize
in the books the amount of collectibles from real property taxes under
General Fund and Special Education Fund in accordance with Section
20 of MNGAS to reflect the actual collectibles from real property taxes
in the financial statements of the Municipality;
b. Instruct the Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the OIC-
Municipal Treasurer an assessment roll containing the list of all persons
whose real property have been newly assessed or reassessed and the
values of such property, and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers (SL)
of the RPT/SET Receivables to monitor tax collections and
delinquencies.

We recommended that Management require the OIC - Municipal Treasurer
to deposit intact all his collections with the authorized government depository bank
daily or not later than the next banking day to safeguard government funds from
possible risk of loss, misuse or misapplication. Further, submit a written
explanation why the collections were not deposited intact within the reglementary
period.

We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the personnel concerned to regularly conduct Physical Inventory
of the Inventory account and reconcile the same with the accounting
records and the GSO;
b. Require the Accounting unit to account for the inventories using the
perpetual inventory method and maintain supply ledger cards;
c. Instruct the GSO to regularly prepare the Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued (SSMI) and forward to Accounting Unit to serve as basis
in the preparation of JEV and recording of issuances;
d. Require the GSO to maintain stock cards and record deliveries and
issuances therein;
e. Adhere to the general process in handling and accounting for Inventories
and Supplies and Materials prescribed in Manual on the New
Government Accounting System in order to strengthen the internal
control of the agency; and
f. Purchase supplies and materials needed for the quarter based on the
approved annual procurement program, at the beginning of the year and
of each subsequent quarters thru public bidding pursuant to Republic
Act No. 9184 and its IRR.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC – Municipal
Treasurer to issue official receipt every end of the year on the unused Petty Cash
Fund account in order to acknowledge the amount returned. We also recommended
that the OIC – Municipal Treasurer refund the unused balance of Petty Cash Fund
amounting to ₱1,653,303.67 and new cash advance shall be set up in the following
year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to refrain from utilizing Petty
Cash Fund for the payment of regular expenses, instead issue checks to
the payees concerned as prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002;
b. Instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of paying
transactions with specific purpose from PCF, instead a cash advance shall
be drawn for such purpose;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to
separate the recording and reporting of the cash advances with specific
purpose from the petty cash fund; and
d. Instruct the Petty Cash Custodian to use the Petty Cash Voucher in the
disbursement of the fund in conformity with Section 48, Volume I of
Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local
Government Unit.

We recommended that Management advise the Municipal Accountant to
reclassify and present the current portion of the Loans Payable - Domestic account
in the Statement of Financial Position as current liability in accordance with the
aforementioned accounting standards for fair presentation of the account in the
financial statements.

We recommended that Management require the BAC Secretariat and
Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the aforementioned lacking
documents to the Audit Team to verify the regularity and validity of the
transactions and to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension.
We further recommended that Management instruct the Municipal
Accountant to exercise prudence and diligence in the performance of his duties and
responsibilities being accountable and responsible person in examining
completeness of the required documents of the transactions of the agency before
affixing his signature on the disbursement voucher and before effecting payment.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to:
a. Ensure all transactions involving procurement of goods undertaken thru
shopping or small value procurement be supported with Request for
Quotation indicating therein the Approved Budget of the Contract; and
b. Require the suppliers to indicate their PhilGEPS Registration Number on
RFQs upon submission to the BAC.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Agriculturist to:
a. Prepare and submit the Inventory of Breeding Stocks every reporting
date and provide information on the prevailing market price and
prevailing cost to sell of the said assets to the Municipal Accountant
which will serve as basis in the revaluation of the account in the books of
the agency; and
b. Furnish the Municipal Accountant with documents/records relative to
additions, distribution, reproduction and death of the Breeding Stocks to
properly reflect the correct balance of the account in the financial
statements.

We recommended that Management properly accomplish all purchase
orders to provide the necessary information and basis in determining the reliability of

No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal

We further recommended that Management submit additional documents as
basis in the payment of financial assistance given to BNS-honorarium and

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare and submit to the Head of the Procuring

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to enforce collection of receivables from the employees concerned to

cash advances in compliance with the existing rules and regulations; and

officers/ employees identified in the schedule of cash advances and to
reflect any adjustments in the books of accounts, if any.

Accountant and Municipal Treasure to retrieve all necessary documents which will

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:
a. Create an Inventory Committee and require the same to conduct
physical inventory and render report thereon as required under Section
124, NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and to regularly monitor the
progress of the inventory taking;
b. Require the Inventory Committee, the General Services Office and the
Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile all the inventory reports with the
books of accounts; and
c. Require the designated Property Officer to maintain property cards and
update them periodically to aid in the conduct of physical count and to solve
or minimize the perennial of unreconciled Accounting and Property records.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to
observe strictly the schedule of rendition of accounts in accordance with the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and submit to the Auditor for post-
audit, so that the Auditor could perform timely audit of the financial
transactions of the Agency.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the following suggested measures:
a. Deposit all collections intact with the authorized government
depository bank as often as necessary; and
b. Stop immediately the practice of retaining collections to defray
operational expenses.

We have recommended that Management:
a. Ensure the strict compliance with the provisions of Republic Act No.
9184 in hiring consultants to avoid unnecessary expenses; and
b. To revisits its organizational structure, staffing pattern and service
requirement to assess the necessity of hiring consultants.

We have recommended that Management stop utilizing Special Education Fund to
expenditures of registration fee of BSP and GSP as it is not of one of the authorized
activities provided in Sections 272 and 100(c) of Republic Act No. 7160.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to use the prescribed form of the Report of Checks Issued so that the
Municipal Accountant could draw Journal Entry Vouchers based on the said
reports and submit to the COA together with the duly paid Disbursement
Vouchers, supporting documents and cancelled checks in compliance with the
aforecited regulation.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to use the prescribed form of the Report of Disbursement so that the
Municipal Accountant could draw Journal Entry Vouchers based on the said
reports and submit to the COA together with the duly paid Disbursement
Vouchers and supporting documents in compliance with the aforecited
regulation.

We have recommended that Management submit immediately the aforementioned
lacking documents to the Office of the Auditor to enable this office to verify the
regularity and validity of the payment.
We have further recommended that the Municipal Accountant exercise prudence and
diligence in exercising his duties and responsibilities being accountable and
responsible person in examining voluminous transactions of the agency before
payments are made.

We have recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to enforce liquidation of cash
advances on the prescribed frequency and impose sanctions by
withholding their salaries pursuant to Section 4.1.3, 5.1.3and 5.9 of
COA Circular 97-002;
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare aging of dormant
receivables, unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers on a
quarterly basis;
c. Management to exert extra effort to locate the whereabouts of the
accountable officers and cause the liquidation of the long outstanding
cash advances in compliance with the existing rules and regulations;
and
d. Request for Write-Off for the outstanding cash advances of
accountable officers who were already been deceased and remained
dormant for more than ten (10) years, pursuant to COA Circular No.
20016-008 in order that the financial statements of the Municipality
would be fairly presented.

We have recommended that Management strictly comply the provisions
of the abovementioned circular in order to avoid suspension or disallowance of
the transaction.

We have recommended that Management require the Municipal
Accountant to strictly observe COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the accounting
and reporting of the 5% LDRRMF. Also, the MDRRMO shall submit the
Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the
end of the month to the COA Auditor.

This is a reiteration of previous year’s audit finding, we recommended that
Management formulate effective strategies to achieve the waste diversion
percentage as targeted in the ensuing years in compliance to Section 20 of R.A.
9003. Likewise, assist the barangays financially and technically in order to
establish and sustain the operation of Material Recovery Facility (MRF). The
establishment of MRFs would help reduce waste to be hauled and transported to
the sanitary landfill, thus, minimizing the cost of hauling, fuel and expenses for
the garbage disposal.

We have recommended that Management instruct the Municipal
Engineer to comply the above mentioned regulation in order to avoid issuance of
suspension and/or disallowance.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant exercise prudence and
diligence in exercising his duties and responsibilities being accountable and
responsible person in examining voluminous transactions of the agency before
payments are made to prevent the suspension of the same in audit.

We have recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the BAC
Chairman furnish copies of perfected contract and each of all the documents
forming part thereof to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days
upon perfection of the contract for review and evaluation thereof.

We have reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management observe fully the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. Except for petty cash fund, require the Accountable Officer to refund any unexpended balance to the cashier/collecting officer, who will issue the necessary official receipt. As to the cash advances of former officials and employees of the municipality and national government agencies who resigned, retired, and transferred, we recommended further, that Management should be persistent in sending demand letters and file appropriate legal action, if warranted to those who have failed to liquidate the overdue cash advances including those who are no longer in the government service.

observations.reason: Some officers already separated from the agency
